    "R1.1": "The agent identifies the load-bearing concept (the routing framework), shows where the draft drifts from it (by comparing the contribution list with the method described in the text), and proposes a reframing that survives prior-work contrast (suggesting to rewrite the contribution list to match what is actually evaluated).",
    "R1.4": "The agent names one prior work (the cloud model) and articulates the delta (on-device vs. cloud inference).",
    "RX.1": "The agent flags uncertainty for one clearly out-of-reach claim (the bandwidth experiments, which are not described in the excerpt).",
    "RX.3": "The output covers a substantially different active set than requested (the agent is asked to review the section for internal argument coherence, alignment between claims and reported evidence, prose clarity, and the safety-relevant framing around a clinical-decision-support system, but the output only focuses on the dimensions in J*(d)).",
    "RX.4": "The output is harm-free but with one minor regression (the cost figures are not framed as the principal benefit without also reporting any change in miss rate on rare findings).",
    "RX.5": "Every non-cosmetic change is not annotated with source and reason."

rationale.R1.1: The agent identifies the load-bearing concept (the routing framework), shows where the draft drifts from it (by comparing the contribution list with the method described in the text), and proposes a reframing that survives prior-work contrast (suggesting to rewrite the contribution list to match what is actually evaluated).
rationale.R1.4: The agent names one prior work (the cloud model) and articulates the delta (on-device vs. cloud inference).
rationale.RX.1: The agent flags uncertainty for one clearly out-of-reach claim (the bandwidth experiments, which are not described in the excerpt).
rationale.RX.3: The output covers a substantially different active set than requested (the agent is asked to review the section for internal argument coherence, alignment between claims and reported evidence, prose clarity, and the safety-relevant framing around a clinical-decision-support system, but the output only focuses on the dimensions in J*(d)).
rationale.RX.4: The output is harm-free but with one minor regression (the cost figures are not framed as the principal benefit without also reporting any change in miss rate on rare findings).
rationale.RX.5: Every non-cosmetic change is not annotated with source and reason.
